script:
export const meta = {
  name: 'race-sota-push',
  description: 'Close the RACE gap to SOTA: diagnose per-dimension where POLARIS loses (current 0.2685 vs ~0.48 frontier), re-score with best compose + multi-task, target the weakest dimension. Honest, measured.',
  phases: [
    { title: 'Diagnose', detail: 'per-dimension breakdown of the 0.2685 + what the reference does better' },
    { title: 'True number', detail: 'best-compose re-render + 3 more DRB tasks for a real average' },
    { title: 'Target', detail: 'Fable: the single highest-leverage fix to climb toward SOTA', model: 'fable' },
  ],
}

const CTX = `
POLARIS must hit SOTA on RACE (DeepResearch Bench, github.com/Ayanami0730/deep_research_bench).
RACE = reference-based LLM judge (openai/gpt-5.5), 4 DIMENSIONS with per-task dynamic weights:
Comprehensiveness, Insight/Depth, Instruction-Following, Readability. Frontier systems (ChatGPT/Gemini
deep research, FS-Researcher arXiv 2602.01566) score ~0.45-0.50. POLARIS's FIRST real number:
DRB task72 overall = 0.2685 (faithfulness PASS) — WELL BELOW SOTA. That report was composed by the
OUTLINE wheel's OWN compose, NOT the improved compose_fix (16-way concurrency). Proof artifacts +
scorer live in /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent (outputs/, scripts/score_report_race.py); the agentic
corpus is data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.json (329 baskets, 'Generative AI's Impact on Employment').
Best compose = /home/polaris/wt/compose_fix (sharded, judge/writer 16-way, glm-5.2 max tokens).

HONESTY: report the REAL per-dimension numbers. If a dimension is genuinely weak, say so. Do NOT tune
the judge to inflate the score. The goal is a TRUE climb toward 0.48, not a flattering number.
Source the OpenRouter key: set -a; . /workspace/POLARIS/.env; set +a  (never print it). Watch cost —
score EXISTING renders where possible; a full new render only when needed. If 429/hang, say so.
`

const DIM_SCHEMA = {
  type: 'object', required: ['dimensions', 'weakest', 'why'],
  properties: {
    overall: { type: 'string' },
    dimensions: { type: 'object', description: 'comprehensiveness/insight/instruction_following/readability -> score + weight, REAL numbers' },
    weakest: { type: 'string', description: 'the dimension where POLARIS loses the most points (score x weight gap)' },
    why: { type: 'string', description: 'concretely, what the reference report does that POLARIS does not — read both' },
    quick_wins: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' } },
  },
}
const NUM_SCHEMA = {
  type: 'object', required: ['scores', 'mean'],
  properties: {
    scores: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'object', properties: { task: { type: 'string' }, overall: { type: 'string' }, compose: { type: 'string', description: 'best or outline-own' } } } },
    mean: { type: 'string', description: 'mean RACE overall across the tasks scored' },
    best_vs_own_compose: { type: 'string', description: 'did the best compose (compose_fix) score higher than the outline-own compose on the same task?' },
    notes: { type: 'string' },
  },
}

phase('Diagnose')
const dim = await agent(
  `${CTX}\n\nDIAGNOSE the 0.2685. Find the RACE result JSON for DRB task72 under
/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/outputs (score_report_race.py output). Extract the PER-DIMENSION
scores + weights (comprehensiveness, insight, instruction-following, readability). Identify the
WEAKEST dimension by lost points (weight x (1 - score)). Then READ both the POLARIS report AND the
task72 REFERENCE report and say concretely what the reference does that POLARIS does not (depth?
coverage? structure? specific analysis?). Give real numbers.`,
  { label: 'race:diagnose-dimensions', phase: 'Diagnose', schema: DIM_SCHEMA },
)

phase('True number')
const num = await agent(
  `${CTX}\n\nGet the TRUE current number, two ways:
(1) MULTI-TASK: one task is noise. Score POLARIS on 2-3 MORE DRB tasks (pick tasks whose topic the
329-basket AI corpus can actually answer, or the nearest reference tasks the harness supports) with
score_report_race.py, same gpt-5.5 judge, to get a MEAN, not a single point.
(2) BEST-COMPOSE: the 0.2685 used the outline's own compose. If feasible without a huge spend, render
the SAME task72 outline through the best compose (compose_fix, 16-way) and re-score — does the best
compose lift the number? If a full re-render is too costly right now, say so and skip, don't fake it.
Report the scores, the mean, and whether best-compose beats outline-own-compose. Diagnosis context:
${JSON.stringify(dim, null, 2)}`,
  { label: 'race:true-number', phase: 'True number', schema: NUM_SCHEMA },
)

phase('Target')
const plan = await agent(
  `${CTX}\n\nYOU ARE FABLE. Given the REAL per-dimension diagnosis and the multi-task number, decide the
SINGLE highest-leverage move to climb toward SOTA (0.48). Be concrete and honest.

DIAGNOSIS: ${JSON.stringify(dim, null, 2)}
TRUE NUMBER: ${JSON.stringify(num, null, 2)}

Answer: (1) What is our HONEST current RACE standing (mean, per dimension) vs the ~0.48 frontier?
(2) Which ONE dimension, fixed, moves the overall the most — and is that a compose problem (rendering
depth/readability), an outline problem (coverage/structure), a corpus problem (weak sourcing), or a
scorer artifact? (3) The concrete next action for the relevant wheel, file/knob-specific where you can.
(4) Is SOTA realistically reachable with the current corpus+pipeline, or is there a structural ceiling
we must name? Do not flatter — the operator wants the truth about the gap and the real path to close it.`,
  { label: 'race:fable-target', phase: 'Target', model: 'fable', effort: 'high' },
)

log(`RACE SOTA: weakest=${dim?.weakest} | mean=${num?.mean} vs SOTA ~0.48`)
return { dim, num, plan }

dim.why: The reference report (task 72, /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/third_party/deep_research_bench/data/test_data/cleaned_data/reference.jsonl) is a 9,029-word / 33-heading / 8-section literature review with TWO summary tables; POLARIS (/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/third_party/deep_research_bench/data/test_data/raw_data/polaris_agentic_run1.jsonl, the OUTLINE-wheel compose) is 1,655 words / 8 headings / ZERO tables = 18% of reference length. RACE normalizes each dimension as target/(target+reference), so POLARIS's 0.25-0.29 means the reference out-scored it ~2.4-3.0x on every axis. Concretely, four things the reference does that POLARIS does not: (1) INSIGHT (weakest, 2.97x behind): the reference builds explicit theoretical scaffolding (task-based displacement/reinstatement/productivity effects a la Acemoglu-Restrepo, SBTC vs RBTC/routine-bias, substitute/complement/augment) and after every section adds a stepped-back analytical synthesis paragraph ('This implies...', 'the direction of AI innovation itself is a key determinant...'), plus a whole section on the productivity-paradox / J-curve and dedicated 'areas of disagreement' + 'research gaps' sections. POLARIS is a data-dump: disconnected fact sentences each ending in a bracket citation, no cross-study synthesis, no discussion of contradictions/heterogeneity, no 'so what'. (2) COMPREHENSIVENESS (2.43x behind): reference covers theory -> empirics (robotics, AI-specific, meta-analyses, firm-level) -> sectoral disruptions (manufacturing/logistics, healthcare/finance/transport, creative/marketing/service) -> wages/inequality/skills -> future outlook -> policy (education, safety nets, taxation, regulation) -> conclusion. POLARIS has 5 thin sections mislabeled with CLINICAL-TRIAL template headers ('Efficacy', 'Safety', 'Comparative', 'Long-term Outcomes') and no intro, no policy section, no conclusion. (3) INSTRUCTION-FOLLOWING (2.96x behind): task 72 demands 'only high-quality, English-language JOURNAL articles', yet POLARIS cites MIT Sloan ideas blog [2], a Wharton budget-model blog [17], articleoneadvisors.com [9], abacademies [13], and OECD/ILO/IMF working papers/reports (many tier UNKNOWN/T3/T4) - the reference's Table 1 cites named journals (JPE, REStat, JLE, Res Policy, IJM). POLARIS also violates the prompt's mandated summary table (the embedded prompt even specified exact column headers; it produced none). Worse, POLARIS was composed against a DIFFERENT query (its own title/prompt is 'Generative AI's impact on employment, research before June 2023, positive/negative/challenges/opportunities') than the RACE task-72 prompt it is scored against, and it ends with a 'Methods'/'Limitations' block dumping its OWN pipeline internals (corpus filename cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.json, tier distribution T1=4%, telemetry 'contradictions_detected', 'not_comparable_pairings', glm-5.2 generator) - meta-commentary about the tool that a literature review must never contain. (4) READABILITY (2.52x behind, POLARIS's least-bad dim): reference uses clean numbered hierarchy, topic sentences, bulleted mechanisms and tables; POLARIS uses run-on citation-stuffed paragraphs under wrong headers.
dim.overall: Verified real numbers from /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/third_party/deep_research_bench/results/race/polaris_agentic_run1/ (race_result.txt + raw_results.jsonl); dimension weights from data/criteria_data/criteria.jsonl task-72 'dimension_weight' = {comprehensiveness 0.29, insight 0.32, instruction_following 0.25, readability 0.14}. Overall = sum(weight*score) = 0.268 (matches reported 0.2685). Ranked by lost points (weight*(1-score)): insight 0.2395 > comprehensiveness 0.2055 > instruction_following 0.1868 > readability 0.1003. Insight is weakest because it carries BOTH the highest weight (0.32) AND the lowest score (0.2516) - the reference scored ~3x POLARIS there. NOTE: this 0.2685 is the OUTLINE wheel's OWN compose (polaris_agentic_run1, 12,985 chars), NOT the improved compose_fix; a like-for-like re-score of a compose_fix render on task 72 would be the honest next measurement. No new render was run (cost/honesty); this is analysis of the existing scored artifact.
quick_wins: Kill the clinical-template outline (Efficacy/Safety/Comparative/Long-term Outcomes) and use topic-shaped sections: Intro+4IR framing, Theoretical frameworks, Empirical findings, Sectoral disruptions, Wages/inequality/skills, Policy, Conclusion+research gaps - directly lifts comprehensiveness and instruction-following

Add 1-2 structured summary tables (study x geography x methodology x employment/wage findings; and policy recommendations) - reference has two, POLARIS has zero, and task-72's embedded prompt explicitly mandated a table
Strip the pipeline-internals 'Methods'/'Limitations' block (corpus filename, tier %, telemetry fields, glm-5.2) - meta-commentary about the tool tanks readability and instruction-following

Enforce journal-only source filtering for this task: drop MIT Sloan/Wharton/advisor blogs and org reports (tier UNKNOWN/T3/T4), cite named peer-reviewed journals - task-72 constraint is 'only high-quality English-language journal articles'

Raise insight: after each evidence cluster, add a synthesis paragraph naming the mechanism, reconciling contradictory findings (e.g. meta-analytic near-zero-but-heterogeneous wage effects), and stating implications - not just citation-stuffed fact lists
Expand length ~4-5x (target ~7-9k words) so coverage can physically approach the 9,029-word reference; current 1,655 words caps comprehensiveness

Re-run the score against the EXACT task-72 prompt with a report actually composed for that prompt (restructuring impact of AI, 4IR framing) - the scored render answered a different embedded query, which structurally caps instruction-following

scores.task: 72 (AI/labor lit review) — outline-own compose, polaris_agentic_run1 baseline
72 — BEST compose (compose_fix iter4, glm-5.2, 16-way), same AI/labor task

num.mean: 0.2630 (outline-own compose across 5 distinct DRB tasks: 72=0.2530, 75=0.2839, 76=0.2646, 78=0.2107, 90=0.3026). Range 0.211–0.303. The original single-point 0.2685 (task72) was representative, NOT a lucky high.
num.best_vs_own_compose: YES. On the SAME task 72, best compose (compose_fix iter4) = 0.3023 beat outline-own = 0.2685 (+0.0338) and the outline vm render = 0.2530 (+0.0493). Best compose improved EVERY dimension vs the 0.2685 baseline: Comprehensiveness 0.2915->0.3391 (+0.048, driven by length 1655->5482 words), Insight 0.2516->0.2875 (+0.036), Instruction-Following 0.2527->0.2775 (+0.025), Readability 0.2839->0.2966 (+0.013). Honest caveat: even best compose at 0.3023 is still FAR below SOTA (~0.45-0.50) and below 0.5 parity with the reference — the 9,029-word reference still outscores POLARIS ~1.5-2x on every axis, and Instruction-Following (0.2775) remains the laggard because the best-compose report still answers its own embedded query ('impact of Generative AI on the future labor market') rather than the exact task-72 prompt (restructuring/4IR/journal-only) and still has ZERO summary tables.
num.notes:
METHOD (honest, no judge tuning): same official RACE harness (third_party/deep_research_bench/deepresearch_bench_race.py) via scripts/score_report_race.py, same judge openai/gpt-5.5, reference-based (overall = target/(target+reference), 0.5 = parity). Per-task dynamic dimension weights unchanged. RACE's built-in article-cleaning step was applied identically to target and reference. No 429s, no hangs; all 6 runs exited 0.

ARTIFACTS SCORED (all PRE-EXISTING renders; no new compose run — cost-honest):
- Best compose: extracted assembled_report_md from /workspace/compose_wt/outputs/s5_gear_iter4/cp5_generation_snapshot.json (acceptance=True, iter4, glm-5.2, 16-way) -> /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/outputs/best_compose_task72/report.md (38,210 chars / 5,482 words). This IS the compose_fix best-compose render of the AI/labor task, so re-scoring it (rather than a fresh $-heavy render) gives the like-for-like best-vs-own comparison the task asked for.
- Outline-family multi-task: /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/outputs/audits/I-redact-001/vm_data/drb_{72,75,76,78,90}/report.md (each answers its own task's prompt with a topic-appropriate corpus). NOTE: these vm_data renders are byte-identical between the outline_agent and compose_fix worktrees (just copied), so they do NOT constitute a second best-vs-own axis; they are one outline-family set used only for the multi-task mean.

RESULT FILES: /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/third_party/deep_research_bench/results/race/{polaris_best_compose_t72,polaris_vm_t72,polaris_vm_t75,polaris_vm_t76,polaris_vm_t78,polaris_vm_t90}/race_result.txt. Original baseline: results/race/polaris_agentic_run1/race_result.txt = 0.2685.

PER-DIMENSION HONESTY (weakest dims persist across the board): outline-own is uniformly ~0.21-0.30 on every axis on every task = reference beats POLARIS ~2-3x everywhere. Weakest single task = 78 Parkinson's (0.2107, thin 1,852-word render, IF only 0.1855). Strongest = 90 ADAS liability (0.3026) and 75 (0.2839). Insight and Instruction-Following stay the two hardest dimensions even for best compose. Length is the biggest lever seen: the +0.048 comprehensiveness jump tracked the 1,655->5,482 word increase, but coverage still caps out well under the 9,029-word reference and neither compose produced the mandated summary table.

BOTTOM LINE: True POLARIS level = ~0.263 mean (outline-own, multi-task), lifting to 0.302 on task72 with the best compose. Both are WELL BELOW the ~0.48 SOTA target. Best compose is a real, consistent improvement (+0.034 on task72, every dimension up) but not a step-change; closing to 0.48 needs the structural fixes the diagnosis named (topic-shaped sections, summary tables, journal-only sourcing, per-cluster synthesis for insight, ~4-5x length, and composing against the EXACT task prompt to unlock instruction-following).

result.plan:
All numbers below re-verified against the on-disk result files (`/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/third_party/deep_research_bench/results/race/*/race_result.txt`, criteria weights confirmed in `data/criteria_data/criteria.jsonl` task 72).

## (1) Honest standing

- **True multi-task level: mean 0.263** (outline-own compose, 5 distinct DRB tasks: 72=0.2530, 75=0.2839, 76=0.2646, 78=0.2107, 90=0.3026). The 0.2685 single-point was representative, not lucky.
- **Best we have ever scored: 0.3023** (compose_fix iter4, task 72 only). Verified per-dimension: Comp 0.3391, Insight 0.2875, IF 0.2775, Read 0.2966.
- **Gap to frontier (~0.48): −0.18 overall.** Since RACE is target/(target+reference) with 0.5 = parity, our 0.25–0.34 per-dimension scores mean the 9,029-word reference beats us **1.9–3.0x on every axis, on every task**. There is no strong dimension; readability is merely least-bad. compose_fix is a real, uniform improvement (+0.034, all four dims up) but not a step-change.

## (2) The one dimension, and what kind of problem it is

**Insight** is the arithmetic answer: highest weight (0.32) x lowest score (0.2516) = 0.2395 lost points, the most of any dimension. Moving insight alone to reference parity (0.5) adds **+0.079 overall** (vs +0.062 IF, +0.060 comp, +0.030 read). But be honest about the arithmetic: **no single dimension fixed reaches 0.48** — from 0.3023, insight-to-parity only gets ~0.37. The frontier requires all four near parity.

**Root-cause classification — it is an outline + compose problem, jointly, and provably not a scorer artifact:**

- **Not scorer:** official harness, same judge, 6 clean runs, reproduces across 2 composes and 5 tasks; article-cleaning applied identically to both sides.
- **Outline problem (the upstream cause):** I confirmed `/home/polaris/wt/compose_fix/config/section_prompts/` contains only `_registry.yaml` and `clinical.yaml` — there is **no economics/labor section-prompt pack**, which is exactly why an AI/labor lit review got "Efficacy/Safety/Comparative/Long-term Outcomes" headers. And the iter4 launcher pins `CP4=/workspace/POLARIS/outputs/s4_gear/cp4_outline_snapshot.json` — an outline built from POLARIS's **own embedded query**, not the verbatim task-72 prompt. That mismatch structurally caps IF (0.2775 even at best) and misshapes comprehensiveness.
- **Compose problem (the insight-specific cause):** the writer emits citation-stuffed fact sentences with zero cross-study synthesis, dumps its contradiction telemetry into a "Methods" block instead of *using* it, and produced zero tables despite the prompt mandating one.
- **Corpus problem (partial, real):** T1 share ~4% in `cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.json` against a "journal-only" task constraint — filtering alone cannot fix IF if the journal-grade material isn't in the corpus.

## (3) The single highest-leverage move

**Make the verbatim DRB task prompt the binding contract for the outline AND compose wheels, then re-render task 72 with compose_fix iter5.** This is one move that lifts three dimensions at once (IF directly; comp and insight via task-shaped structure), where a pure insight fix lifts one. Concretely:

1. **Outline wheel:** regenerate cp4 from the exact task-72 prompt (restructuring/4IR/journal-only/mandated table). Add a `labor_economics.yaml` (or wire the existing `/home/polaris/wt/compose_fix/config/domain_packs/economics.yaml`) into `config/section_prompts/_registry.yaml` so template selection stops defaulting to clinical. Target 8 topic-shaped sections: intro/4IR framing → theory (displacement/reinstatement, SBTC vs RBTC) → empirics → sectoral → wages/inequality/skills → policy → disagreements+gaps → conclusion.
2. **Compose wheel** (`launch_compose_gear_iter5.sh`, pin the NEW cp4): (a) per-section mandatory synthesis paragraph fed by the existing `contradictions_detected`/`not_comparable_pairings` telemetry — that signal already exists and is currently wasted as meta-commentary; (b) two mandated summary tables with the prompt's exact column headers; (c) hard-strip the pipeline-internals Methods/Limitations block; (d) length target ~8–9k words (token headroom already maxed at `PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_MAX_TOKENS=131072`; the knob to raise is section count via the new cp4, not tokens); (e) journal-tier citation filter at render time.
3. Re-score with `scripts/score_report_race.py`, task 72 first, then the 5-task set for the honest mean.

## (4) Is 0.48 reachable? — the truth

**Not with the current corpus + pipeline as-is. There is a structural ceiling and it has two layers.**

- **Layer 1 (fixable by the move above, worth maybe +0.06–0.10):** wrong-prompt composition, clinical template, no tables, no synthesis, 1.6–5.5k words vs 9k reference. Fixing all of it plausibly gets task-72 to **~0.36–0.40**. That is the honest expected value — not 0.48.
- **Layer 2 (the named ceiling):** (a) the **corpus**: ~4% T1 cannot satisfy a journal-only constraint; the discovery wheel must retrieve peer-reviewed journal articles per-task, or IF and insight both cap out — insight parity requires reconciling *named published studies*, which requires having them; (b) **per-task everything**: the 0.263 mean shows every task needs its own prompt-contracted outline + topic corpus; a shared pipeline template family reused across domains is what produced the 0.211 Parkinson's floor. SOTA systems at 0.45–0.50 achieve near-parity with 9k-word expert references on all four axes simultaneously — that requires per-task retrieval depth we do not currently have.

**Bottom line:** we are at 0.263 mean / 0.302 best, roughly 55–63% of the frontier. The single move (task-prompt-contracted outline + iter5 compose) is the biggest available step and should be run next; expect ~0.36–0.40, not 0.48. Closing the final ~0.08–0.12 requires the discovery/corpus wheel to deliver journal-grade, task-specific sources — that is the structural constraint, and no compose tuning will paper over it.

logs: RACE SOTA: weakest=insight | mean=0.2630 (outline-own compose across 5 distinct DRB tasks: 72=0.2530, 75=0.2839, 76=0.2646, 78=0.2107, 90=0.3026). Range 0.211–0.303. The original single-point 0.2685 (task72) was representative, NOT a lucky high. vs SOTA ~0.48
summary: Close the RACE gap to SOTA: diagnose per-dimension where POLARIS loses (current 0.2685 vs ~0.48 frontier), re-score with best compose + multi-task, target the weakest dimension. Honest, measured.

workflowProgress.resultPreview:
All numbers below re-verified against the on-disk result files (`/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/third_party/deep_research_bench/results/race/*/race_result.txt`, criteria weights confirmed in `data/criteria_data/criteria.jsonl` task 72).

## (1) Honest standing

- **True multi-task level: mean 0.263** (outline-own compose, 5 distinct DRB tasks: 72=0.2530, 75=0.2839, 76=0.2646, 78=0.2107, 90=0.3026)…
